message:
fix(session): fsync parent directories after atomic renames for crash safety

Add sync_parent_dir() calls after every fs::rename() in the session
store to ensure directory entries are durable on disk before returning
success. Without these fsyncs, a power loss or kernel crash between
the rename and the next natural sync could leave the directory pointing
at the old (or no) file, silently losing a checkpoint, manifest, or
segment index that the caller believed was persisted.

Affected code paths:
- write_checkpoint: fsync after renaming tmp checkpoint file
- compact (index rewrite): best-effort fsync after index rename
- write_manifest: fsync after renaming tmp manifest
- rebuild_index: fsync after renaming rebuilt index into place

The sync_parent_dir helper is Unix-only (opens the parent dir and calls
sync_all); on non-Unix platforms it is a no-op, matching the existing
platform-specific durability expectations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
